revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社| 所在地=奈良県葛城市當麻町加守1045| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|51|N|135|41|18|E|...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社| 所在地=奈良県葛城市當麻町加守1045| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|51|N|135|41|18|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=天羽雷命| 社格=式内社（大）・村社| 本殿=三間社[[流造]]| 例祭=[[10月17日]]}}
'''葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社'''（かつらぎしずりにいますあまはいかづちのみことじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[葛城市]][[當麻町]]の[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。単に'''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）とも呼ばれる。
==祭神== 天羽雷命（あまはいかづちのみこと）を主祭神とし、右殿に摂社・掃守神社（天忍人命）、左殿に摂社・二上神社（[[国魂|大国魂命]]）を配祀する。
天羽雷命は各地に機織や裁縫の技術を伝えた[[倭文氏]]の祖神で、当社は日本各地にある倭文神社の根本の神社とされる。
天忍人命は[[ウガヤフキアエズ|彦波渚武鵜草葺不合命]]生誕のときにその胎便（「蟹」と呼ばれる）を掃除したという神で、その子孫は蟹守（かもり）氏（掃部、加守、狩森などとも）と称した。当社の社家も蟹守氏である。附近の「加守」という地名も蟹守に由来するもので、当社は江戸時代まで「加守明神」と呼ばれていた。
大国魂命は二上山山頂にある[[葛木二上神社]]の祭神である。山頂の二上神社の遥拝所もしくは[[里宮]]的な存在であったとみられる。
==歴史== 創建の年代等は不詳である。文献の初出は、『[[日本三代実録]]』の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）正月27日条、当社に[[従五位上]]の[[神階]]を授けるという記述である。[[延喜式神名帳]]では「[[大和国]][[葛下郡]] 葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社」と記載され、大社に列している（ただし、葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社については葛城市新庄町の[[博西神社]]も論社とされている）。
明治6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列格し、明治40年（[[1907年]]）に神饌幣帛料供進社に指定された。
==外照リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/yamato/html/030601-01.html 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://kamnavi.jp/as/katuragi/kzkamori.htm 葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社]（神奈備）
{{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきしすりにいますあまはいかつちのみことしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:葛城市]]

revision.text:

revision.comment: /* 祭神 */ [[倭文神社]]
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社| 所在地=奈良県葛城市加守1045| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|51|N|135|41|18|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=天羽雷命| 社格=式内社（大）・村社| 本殿=三間社[[流造]]| 画像=[[Image:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja1.jpg|220px]]| 例祭=[[10月17日]]}}
'''葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社'''（かつらきしとりにいますあめのはいかづちのみことじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]（旧[[當麻町]]）の[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。単に'''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）とも呼ばれる。
==祭神== 天羽雷命（あまはいかづちのみこと）を主祭神とし、右殿に摂社・掃守神社（天忍人命）、左殿に摂社・二上神社（[[国魂|大国魂命]]）を配祀する。
天羽雷命は各地に機織や裁縫の技術を伝えた[[倭文氏]]の祖神で、当社は日本各地にある[[倭文神社]]の根本の神社とされる。
天忍人命は[[ウガヤフキアエズ|彦波渚武鵜草葺不合命]]生誕のときにその胎便（「蟹」と呼ばれる）を掃除したという神で、その子孫は蟹守（かもり）氏（掃部、加守、狩森などとも）と称した。当社の社家も蟹守氏である。附近の「加守」という地名も蟹守に由来するもので、当社は江戸時代まで「加守明神」と呼ばれていた。
大国魂命は二上山山頂にある[[葛木二上神社]]の祭神である。山頂の二上神社の遥拝所もしくは[[里宮]]的な存在であったとみられる。
==歴史== 創建の年代等は不詳である。文献の初出は、『[[日本三代実録]]』の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）正月27日条、当社に[[従五位|従五位上]]の[[神階]]を授けるという記述である。[[延喜式神名帳]]では「[[大和国]][[葛下郡]] 葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社」と記載され、大社に列している（ただし、葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社については葛城市新庄町の[[博西神社]]も論社とされている）。
明治6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列格し、明治40年（[[1907年]]）に神饌幣帛料供進社に指定された。
==外照リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/yamato/html/030601-01.html 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://kamnavi.jp/as/katuragi/kzkamori.htm 葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社]（神奈備） {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきしとりにいますあめのはいかつちのみことしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:葛城市]]

==外照リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/yamato/html/030601-01.html 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://kamnavi.jp/as/katuragi/kzkamori.htm 葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社]（神奈備） {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきしとりにいますあめのはいかつちのみことしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:葛城市]]

天羽雷命は各地に機織や裁縫の技術を伝えた[[倭文氏]]の祖神で、当社は日本各地にある[[倭文神社]]の根本の神社とされる。
天忍人命は[[ウガヤフキアエズ|彦波渚武鵜草葺不合命]]生誕のときにその胎便（「蟹」と呼ばれる）を掃除したという神で、その子孫は蟹守（かもり）氏（掃部、加守、狩森などとも）と称した。当社の社家も蟹守氏である。附近の「加守」という地名も蟹守に由来するもので、当社は江戸時代まで「加守明神」と呼ばれていた。
大国魂命は二上山山頂にある[[葛木二上神社]]の祭神である。山頂の二上神社の遥拝所もしくは[[里宮]]的な存在であったとみられる。
==歴史== 創建の年代等は不詳である。文献の初出は、『[[日本三代実録]]』の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）正月27日条、当社に[[従五位|従五位上]]の[[神階]]を授けるという記述である。[[延喜式神名帳]]では「[[大和国]][[葛下郡]] 葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社」と記載され、大社に列している（ただし、葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社については葛城市新庄町の[[博西神社]]も論社とされている）。
明治6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列格し、明治40年（[[1907年]]）に神饌幣帛料供進社に指定された。

境内（石段奥に拝殿） |名称=葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]加守1045 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 17.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 36 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=[[天羽槌雄神|天羽雷命]] |創建=不詳 |社格=[[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |例祭=[[10月17日]] }} [[画像:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社'''（かつらきしずりにいますあめのはいかづちのみことじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]の[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓にある[[神社]]である。[[式内大社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。単に'''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）とも呼ばれる。 == 祭神 == [[天羽槌雄神|天羽雷命]]（あまはいかづちのみこと）を主祭神とし、右殿に摂社・掃守神社（[[天忍人命]]）、左殿に摂社・二上神社（[[国魂|大国魂命]]）を配祀する。 天羽雷命は各地に機織や裁縫の技術を伝えた[[倭文氏]]の祖神で、当社は日本各地にある[[倭文神社]]の根本の神社とされる。 天忍人命は[[ウガヤフキアエズ|彦波渚武鵜草葺不合命]]生誕のときにその胎便（「蟹」と呼ばれる）を掃除したという神で、その子孫は蟹守（かもり）氏（掃部、加守、狩森などとも）と称した。当社の社家も蟹守氏である。附近の「加守」という地名も蟹守に由来するもので、当社は江戸時代まで「加守明神」と呼ばれていた。 大国魂命は二上山山頂にある[[葛木二上神社]]の祭神である。山頂の二上神社の遥拝所もしくは里宮的な存在であったとみられる。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建の年代等は不詳である。文献の初出は、『[[日本三代実録]]』の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]条、当社に[[従五位|従五位上]]の[[神階]]を授けるという記述である。[[延喜式神名帳]]では「[[大和国]][[葛下郡]] 葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社」と記載され、大社に列している（ただし、葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社については葛城市新庄町の[[博西神社]]も論社とされている）。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列格し、明治40年（[[1907年]]）に神饌幣帛料供進社に指定された。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下から従五位上（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「葛木倭文天羽雷命神」。 == 境内 == ファイル:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|境内社 == 関連項目 == *[[倭文神社]] *[[倭文氏]] *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきしとりにいますあめのはいかつちのみことしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきしとりにいますあめのはいかつちのみことしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]]

旧[[村社]] |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |例祭=[[10月17日]] }} [[画像:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|境内入口}}]] '''葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社'''（かつらきしとりにいますあめのはいかづちのみことじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]の[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓にある[[神社]]である。[[式内大社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。単に'''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）とも呼ばれる。 == 祭神 == [[天羽槌雄神|天羽雷命]]（あまはいかづちのみこと）を主祭神とし、右殿に摂社・掃守神社（[[天忍人命]]）、左殿に摂社・二上神社（[[国魂|大国魂命]]）を配祀する。 天羽雷命は各地に機織や裁縫の技術を伝えた[[倭文氏]]の祖神で、当社は日本各地にある[[倭文神社]]の根本の神社とされる。 天忍人命は[[ウガヤフキアエズ|彦波渚武鵜草葺不合命]]生誕のときにその胎便（「蟹」と呼ばれる）を掃除したという神で、その子孫は蟹守（かもり）氏（掃部、加守、狩森などとも）と称した。当社の社家も蟹守氏である。附近の「加守」という地名も蟹守に由来するもので、当社は江戸時代まで「加守明神」と呼ばれていた。 大国魂命は二上山山頂にある[[葛木二上神社]]の祭神である。山頂の二上神社の遥拝所もしくは里宮的な存在であったとみられる。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建の年代等は不詳である。文献の初出は、『[[日本三代実録]]』の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]条、当社に[[従五位|従五位上]]の[[神階]]を授けるという記述である。[[延喜式神名帳]]では「[[大和国]][[葛下郡]] 葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社」と記載され、大社に列している（ただし、葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社については葛城市新庄町の[[博西神社]]も論社とされている）。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列格し、明治40年（[[1907年]]）に神饌幣帛料供進社に指定された。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下から従五位上（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「葛木倭文天羽雷命神」。 == 境内 == ファイル:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|境内社 == 関連項目 == *[[倭文神社]] *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきしとりにいますあめのはいかつちのみことしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきしとりにいますあめのはいかつちのみことしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]]

[[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | image = [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Main hall at the top of the stone steps) | name = Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine | address = 1045 Kamori, [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | lon_minutes = 41 | lon_seconds = 18 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | country = Japan | map_type = Japan Nara | deity = {{ill|Ameno Hachio-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} | founded = Unknown | ranking = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Major) - Disputed Shrine
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | honden = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | annual_festival = October 17 }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|ja|葛城市}}, at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo (Nara-Osaka)|ja|二上山}}. It is a **Shikinaisha Taisha (式内大社)**, and its former rank under the **shrine ranking system** was **village shrine (村社)**. It is also commonly known as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社). == Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines **{{ill|Ameno Hachio-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}** (Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto), alongside two auxiliary shrines: * **Right Hall** – Auxiliary shrine: **Sohori Shrine (掃守神社)**, enshrining {{ill|Amano Oshihito no Mikoto|ja|天忍人命}} * **Left Hall** – Auxiliary shrine: **Futakami Shrine (二上神社)**, enshrining {{ill|Okunitama|ja|大国魂命}} Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of the **Shidori clan (倭文氏)**, who spread **weaving and sewing techniques** across Japan. This shrine is considered the **central shrine of all Shidori Shrines (倭文神社)** in Japan. Amano Oshihito no Mikoto is a deity associated with **cleansing impurities**, known for removing the **afterbirth (胎便)** of {{ill|Hiko-nagisa-takeugaya-fuki-aezu no Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, which is referred to as "Kani" (蟹). His descendants were known as the **Kanimori clan (蟹守氏)**, later called **Kamori (加守)**, **Kamon (掃部)**, or **Karimori (狩森)**. The name **"Kamori" (加守)** is preserved in the **local place name**, and until the **Edo period**, the shrine was called "Kamori Myojin" (加守明神). Okunitama is the **deity of the summit shrine of Mount Nijo**, enshrined at **Katsuragi Futakami Shrine (葛木二上神社)**. This shrine may have historically functioned as its **worship site or satomiya (里宮)**. == History == === Historical Overview === The exact date of the shrine's **founding is unknown**. The earliest written record is from the **Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (日本三代実録)**, which states that on **January 27, 859 (Jogan 1)**, the shrine was granted the **rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)**. The **Engishiki Jinmyocho (延喜式神名帳)** lists this shrine as "**Katsuragi Shidori niimasu Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine (葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社)**," classifying it as a **major shrine (大社)**. However, its identity is **disputed with Hakusei Shrine (博西神社) in Shinjō, Katsuragi**. In **1873 (Meiji 6)**, the shrine was designated as a **village shrine** under the modern **shrine ranking system**. Later, in **1907 (Meiji 40)**, it was officially recognized as a **Shinsen Heihakuryō Kusinsha (神饌幣帛料供進社)**, meaning it received **official government offerings**. === Shrine Rankings === * **January 27, 859 (Jogan 1, Ten'an 3)** – Promoted from **Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下)** to **Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)** (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*). - The shrine's name in the text is **"Katsuragi Shidori Amanohaikazuchi no Mikoto" (葛木倭文天羽雷命神)**. == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Sanctuary (Honden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Shrine Auxiliary Building == Access == * **Train:** From [[Kintetsu Railway]]'s [[Minami-Osaka Line]], **get off at Futakami Shrine Station (二上神社口駅)** and walk **10 minutes west**. == See Also == * {{ill|Shidori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, "Shinto and Shrine Historical Archive" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

His descendants were known as the **Kanimori clan (蟹守氏)**, later called **Kamori (加守)**, **Kamon (掃部)**, or **Karimori (狩森)**. The name **"Kamori" (加守)** is preserved in the **local place name**, and until the **Edo period**, the shrine was called "Kamori Myojin" (加守明神). Okunitama is the **deity of the summit shrine of Mount Nijo**, enshrined at **Katsuragi Futakami Shrine (葛木二上神社)**. This shrine may have historically functioned as its **worship site or satomiya (里宮)**. == History == === Historical Overview === The exact date of the shrine's **founding is unknown**. The earliest written record is from the **Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (日本三代実録)**, which states that on **January 27, 859 (Jogan 1)**, the shrine was granted the **rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)**. The **Engishiki Jinmyocho (延喜式神名帳)** lists this shrine as "**Katsuragi Shidori niimasu Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine (葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社)**," classifying it as a **major shrine (大社)**. However, its identity is **disputed with Hakusei Shrine (博西神社) in Shinjō, Katsuragi**. In **1873 (Meiji 6)**, the shrine was designated as a **village shrine** under the modern **shrine ranking system**. Later, in **1907 (Meiji 40)**, it was officially recognized as a **Shinsen Heihakuryō Kusinsha (神饌幣帛料供進社)**, meaning it received **official government offerings**. === Shrine Rankings === * **January 27, 859 (Jogan 1, Ten'an 3)** – Promoted from **Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下)** to **Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)** (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*). - The shrine's name in the text is **"Katsuragi Shidori Amanohaikazuchi no Mikoto" (葛木倭文天羽雷命神)**. == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Sanctuary (Honden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Shrine Auxiliary Building == Access == * **Train:** From [[Kintetsu Railway]]'s [[Minami-Osaka Line]], **get off at Futakami Shrine Station (二上神社口駅)** and walk **10 minutes west**. == See Also == * {{ill|Shidori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, "Shinto and Shrine Historical Archive" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

The '''Engishiki Jinmyocho (延喜式神名帳)''' lists this shrine as "'''Katsuragi Shidori niimasu Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine (葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社)'''," classifying it as a '''major shrine (大社)'''. However, its identity is '''disputed with Hakusei Shrine (博西神社) in Shinjō, Katsuragi'''. In '''1873 (Meiji 6)''', the shrine was designated as a '''village shrine''' under the modern '''shrine ranking system'''. Later, in '''1907 (Meiji 40)''', it was officially recognized as a '''Shinsen Heihakuryō Kusinsha (神饌幣帛料供進社)''', meaning it received '''official government offerings'''. === Shrine Rankings === * '''January 27, 859 (Jogan 1, Ten'an 3)''' – Promoted from '''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下)''' to '''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)''' (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*). - The shrine's name in the text is '''"Katsuragi Shidori Amanohaikazuchi no Mikoto" (葛木倭文天羽雷命神)'''. == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Sanctuary (Honden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Shrine Auxiliary Building == Access == * '''Train:''' From [[Kintetsu Railway]]'s [[Minami-Osaka Line]], '''get off at Futakami Shrine Station (二上神社口駅)''' and walk '''10 minutes west'''. == See Also == * {{ill|Shidori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, "Shinto and Shrine Historical Archive" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

[[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | image = [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Main hall at the top of the stone steps) | name = Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine | address = 1045 Kamori, [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | lon_minutes = 41 | lon_seconds = 18 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | country = Japan | map_type = Japan Nara | deity = {{ill|8=fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Ameno Hachio-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} | founded = Unknown | ranking = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Major) - Disputed Shrine
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | honden = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | annual_festival = October 17 }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|ja|葛城市}}, at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo (Nara-Osaka)|ja|二上山}}. It is a '''Shikinaisha Taisha (式内大社)''', and its former rank under the '''shrine ranking system''' was '''village shrine (村社)'''. It is also commonly known as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社). == Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines '''{{ill|8=fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Ameno Hachio-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}''' (Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto), alongside two auxiliary shrines: * '''Right Hall''' – Auxiliary shrine: '''Sohori Shrine (掃守神社)''', enshrining {{ill|Amano Oshihito no Mikoto|ja|天忍人命}} * '''Left Hall''' – Auxiliary shrine: '''Futakami Shrine (二上神社)''', enshrining {{ill|Okunitama|ja|大国魂命}} Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of the '''Shidori clan (倭文氏)''', who spread '''weaving and sewing techniques''' across Japan. This shrine is considered the '''central shrine of all Shidori Shrines (倭文神社)''' in Japan. Amano Oshihito no Mikoto is a deity associated with '''cleansing impurities''', known for removing the '''afterbirth (胎便)''' of {{ill|Hiko-nagisa-takeugaya-fuki-aezu no Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, which is referred to as "Kani" (蟹). His descendants were known as the '''Kanimori clan (蟹守氏)''', later called '''Kamori (加守)''', '''Kamon (掃部)''', or '''Karimori (狩森)'''. The name '''"Kamori" (加守)''' is preserved in the '''local place name''', and until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was called "Kamori Myojin" (加守明神). Okunitama is the '''deity of the summit shrine of Mount Nijo''', enshrined at '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine (葛木二上神社)'''. This shrine may have historically functioned as its '''worship site or satomiya (里宮)'''. == History == === Historical Overview === The exact date of the shrine's '''founding is unknown'''. The earliest written record is from the '''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (日本三代実録)''', which states that on '''January 27, 859 (Jogan 1)''', the shrine was granted the '''rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)'''. The '''Engishiki Jinmyocho (延喜式神名帳)''' lists this shrine as "'''Katsuragi Shidori niimasu Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine (葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社)'''," classifying it as a '''major shrine (大社)'''. However, its identity is '''disputed with Hakusei Shrine (博西神社) in Shinjō, Katsuragi'''. In '''1873 (Meiji 6)''', the shrine was designated as a '''village shrine''' under the modern '''shrine ranking system'''. Later, in '''1907 (Meiji 40)''', it was officially recognized as a '''Shinsen Heihakuryō Kusinsha (神饌幣帛料供進社)''', meaning it received '''official government offerings'''. === Shrine Rankings === * '''January 27, 859 (Jogan 1, Ten'an 3)''' – Promoted from '''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下)''' to '''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)''' (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*). - The shrine's name in the text is '''"Katsuragi Shidori Amanohaikazuchi no Mikoto" (葛木倭文天羽雷命神)'''. == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Sanctuary (Honden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Shrine Auxiliary Building == Access == * '''Train:''' From [[Kintetsu Railway]]'s [[Minami-Osaka Line]], '''get off at Futakami Shrine Station (二上神社口駅)''' and walk '''10 minutes west'''. == See Also == * {{ill|Shidori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, "Shinto and Shrine Historical Archive" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]]

In '''1873 (Meiji 6)''', the shrine was designated as a '''village shrine''' under the modern '''shrine ranking system'''. Later, in '''1907 (Meiji 40)''', it was officially recognized as a '''Shinsen Heihakuryō Kusinsha (神饌幣帛料供進社)''', meaning it received '''official government offerings'''. === Shrine Rankings === * '''January 27, 859 (Jogan 1, Ten'an 3)''' – Promoted from '''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下)''' to '''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)''' (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*). - The shrine's name in the text is '''"Katsuragi Shidori Amanohaikazuchi no Mikoto" (葛木倭文天羽雷命神)'''. == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Sanctuary (Honden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Shrine Auxiliary Building == Access == * '''Train:''' From [[Kintetsu Railway]]'s [[Minami-Osaka Line]], '''get off at Futakami Shrine Station (二上神社口駅)''' and walk '''10 minutes west'''. == See Also == * {{ill|Shidori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, "Shinto and Shrine Historical Archive" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[sn:Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

[[Category:pages goodbye $1]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Main hall at the top of the stone steps) | name = Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine | address = 1045 Kamori, [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | lon_minutes = 41 | lon_seconds = 18 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | country = Japan | map_type = Japan Nara | deity = {{ill|8=fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Ameno Hachio-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} | founded = Unknown | ranking = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Major) - Disputed Shrine
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | honden = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | annual_festival = October 17 }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|ja|葛城市}}, at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo (Nara-Osaka)|ja|二上山}}. It is a '''Shikinaisha Taisha (式内大社)''', and its former rank under the '''shrine ranking system''' was '''village shrine (村社)'''. It is also commonly known as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社). == Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines '''{{ill|8=fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Ameno Hachio-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}''' (Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto), alongside two auxiliary shrines: * '''Right Hall''' – Auxiliary shrine: '''Sohori Shrine (掃守神社)''', enshrining {{ill|Amano Oshihito no Mikoto|ja|天忍人命}} * '''Left Hall''' – Auxiliary shrine: '''Futakami Shrine (二上神社)''', enshrining {{ill|Okunitama|ja|大国魂命}} Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of the '''Shidori clan (倭文氏)''', who spread '''weaving and sewing techniques''' across Japan. This shrine is considered the '''central shrine of all Shidori Shrines (倭文神社)''' in Japan. Amano Oshihito no Mikoto is a deity associated with '''cleansing impurities''', known for removing the '''afterbirth (胎便)''' of {{ill|Hiko-nagisa-takeugaya-fuki-aezu no Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, which is referred to as "Kani" (蟹). His descendants were known as the '''Kanimori clan (蟹守氏)''', later called '''Kamori (加守)''', '''Kamon (掃部)''', or '''Karimori (狩森)'''. The name '''"Kamori" (加守)''' is preserved in the '''local place name''', and until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was called "Kamori Myojin" (加守明神). Okunitama is the '''deity of the summit shrine of Mount Nijo''', enshrined at '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine (葛木二上神社)'''. This shrine may have historically functioned as its '''worship site or satomiya (里宮)'''. == History == === Historical Overview === The exact date of the shrine's '''founding is unknown'''. The earliest written record is from the '''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (日本三代実録)''', which states that on '''January 27, 859 (Jogan 1)''', the shrine was granted the '''rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)'''. The '''Engishiki Jinmyocho (延喜式神名帳)''' lists this shrine as "'''Katsuragi Shidori niimasu Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine (葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社)'''," classifying it as a '''major shrine (大社)'''. However, its identity is '''disputed with Hakusei Shrine (博西神社) in Shinjō, Katsuragi'''. In '''1873 (Meiji 6)''', the shrine was designated as a '''village shrine''' under the modern '''shrine ranking system'''. Later, in '''1907 (Meiji 40)''', it was officially recognized as a '''Shinsen Heihakuryō Kusinsha (神饌幣帛料供進社)''', meaning it received '''official government offerings'''. === Shrine Rankings === * '''January 27, 859 (Jogan 1, Ten'an 3)''' – Promoted from '''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下)''' to '''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)''' (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*). - The shrine's name in the text is '''"Katsuragi Shidori Amanohaikazuchi no Mikoto" (葛木倭文天羽雷命神)'''. == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Sanctuary (Honden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Shrine Auxiliary Building == Access == * '''Train:''' From [[Kintetsu Railway]]'s [[Minami-Osaka Line]], '''get off at Futakami Shrine Station (二上神社口駅)''' and walk '''10 minutes west'''. == See Also == * {{ill|Shidori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, "Shinto and Shrine Historical Archive" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[sn:Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

The earliest written record is from the '''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (日本三代実録)''', which states that on '''January 27, 859 (Jogan 1)''', the shrine was granted the '''rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)'''. The '''Engishiki Jinmyocho (延喜式神名帳)''' lists this shrine as "'''Katsuragi Shidori niimasu Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine (葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社)'''," classifying it as a '''major shrine (大社)'''. However, its identity is '''disputed with Hakusei Shrine (博西神社) in Shinjō, Katsuragi'''. In '''1873 (Meiji 6)''', the shrine was designated as a '''village shrine''' under the modern '''shrine ranking system'''. Later, in '''1907 (Meiji 40)''', it was officially recognized as a '''Shinsen Heihakuryō Kusinsha (神饌幣帛料供進社)''', meaning it received '''official government offerings'''. === Shrine Rankings === * '''January 27, 859 (Jogan 1, Ten'an 3)''' – Promoted from '''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下)''' to '''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)''' (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*). - The shrine's name in the text is '''"Katsuragi Shidori Amanohaikazuchi no Mikoto" (葛木倭文天羽雷命神)'''. == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Sanctuary (Honden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Shrine Auxiliary Building == Access == * '''Train:''' From [[Kintetsu Railway]]'s [[Minami-Osaka Line]], '''get off at Futakami Shrine Station (二上神社口駅)''' and walk '''10 minutes west'''. == See Also == * {{ill|Shidori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, "Shinto and Shrine Historical Archive" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine[[Category:qq]] {{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine[[Category:qq]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

[[Category:pages goodbye $1]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Main hall at the top of the stone steps) | name = Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine | address = 1045 Kamori, [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | lon_minutes = 41 | lon_seconds = 18 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | country = Japan | map_type = Japan Nara | deity = {{ill|8=fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Ameno Hachio-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} | founded = Unknown | ranking = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Major) - Disputed Shrine
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | honden = Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | annual_festival = October 17 }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|ja|葛城市}}, at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo (Nara-Osaka)|ja|二上山}}. It is a '''Shikinaisha Taisha (式内大社)''', and its former rank under the '''shrine ranking system''' was '''village shrine (村社)'''. It is also commonly known as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社). == Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines '''{{ill|8=fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Ameno Hachio-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}''' (Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto), alongside two auxiliary shrines: * '''Right Hall''' – Auxiliary shrine: '''Sohori Shrine (掃守神社)''', enshrining {{ill|Amano Oshihito no Mikoto|ja|天忍人命}} * '''Left Hall''' – Auxiliary shrine: '''Futakami Shrine (二上神社)''', enshrining {{ill|Okunitama|ja|大国魂命}} Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of the '''Shidori clan (倭文氏)''', who spread '''weaving and sewing techniques''' across Japan. This shrine is considered the '''central shrine of all Shidori Shrines (倭文神社)''' in Japan. Amano Oshihito no Mikoto is a deity associated with '''cleansing impurities''', known for removing the '''afterbirth (胎便)''' of {{ill|Hiko-nagisa-takeugaya-fuki-aezu no Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, which is referred to as "Kani" (蟹). His descendants were known as the '''Kanimori clan (蟹守氏)''', later called '''Kamori (加守)''', '''Kamon (掃部)''', or '''Karimori (狩森)'''. The name '''"Kamori" (加守)''' is preserved in the '''local place name''', and until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was called "Kamori Myojin" (加守明神). Okunitama is the '''deity of the summit shrine of Mount Nijo''', enshrined at '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine (葛木二上神社)'''. This shrine may have historically functioned as its '''worship site or satomiya (里宮)'''. == History == === Historical Overview === The exact date of the shrine's '''founding is unknown'''. The earliest written record is from the '''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (日本三代実録)''', which states that on '''January 27, 859 (Jogan 1)''', the shrine was granted the '''rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)'''. The '''Engishiki Jinmyocho (延喜式神名帳)''' lists this shrine as "'''Katsuragi Shidori niimasu Ameno Haikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine (葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社)'''," classifying it as a '''major shrine (大社)'''. However, its identity is '''disputed with Hakusei Shrine (博西神社) in Shinjō, Katsuragi'''. In '''1873 (Meiji 6)''', the shrine was designated as a '''village shrine''' under the modern '''shrine ranking system'''. Later, in '''1907 (Meiji 40)''', it was officially recognized as a '''Shinsen Heihakuryō Kusinsha (神饌幣帛料供進社)''', meaning it received '''official government offerings'''. === Shrine Rankings === * '''January 27, 859 (Jogan 1, Ten'an 3)''' – Promoted from '''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下)''' to '''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (従五位上)''' (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*). - The shrine's name in the text is '''"Katsuragi Shidori Amanohaikazuchi no Mikoto" (葛木倭文天羽雷命神)'''. == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Main Sanctuary (Honden) File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Shrine Auxiliary Building == Access == * '''Train:''' From [[Kintetsu Railway]]'s [[Minami-Osaka Line]], '''get off at Futakami Shrine Station (二上神社口駅)''' and walk '''10 minutes west'''. == See Also == * {{ill|Shidori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, "Shinto and Shrine Historical Archive" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命}} | established=Unknown | shrine_rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[Mount Nijo (Nara–Osaka)|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文氏}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote worship site for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == === Overview === The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:pages goodbye $1]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文氏}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote worship site for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == === Overview === The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:pages goodbye $1]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}} | established=Unknown | shrine_rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文氏}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote worship site for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == === Overview === The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:pages goodbye $1]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:pages goodbye $1]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}} | established=Unknown | shrine_rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文氏}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == === Overview === The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:pages goodbye $1]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}} | established=Unknown | shrine_rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文氏}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == === Overview === The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:pages goodbye $1]] [[Category:11]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == === Overview === The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - {{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}} | established=Unknown | shrine_rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文氏}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}{{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:11]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}{{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}
