revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}} | established=Unknown | shrine_rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文氏}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year }} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year }} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}

The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=draft}} | established=Unknown | shrine_rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=draft}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=draft}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=draft}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文氏|12=draft}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=draft}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=draft}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=draft}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=draft}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|12=draft}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}

The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=draft}} | established=Unknown | shrine_rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=draft}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=draft}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=draft}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文氏|12=draft}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=draft}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=draft}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=draft}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=draft}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|12=draft}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}

In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=draft}}, is also a candidate for this designation. The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Shidori clan" to "Shizuri clan"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=draft}} | established=Unknown | shrine_rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=draft}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=draft}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=draft}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=draft}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=draft}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=draft}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=draft}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=draft}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|12=draft}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}}

The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=draft}} | established=Unknown | shrine_rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=draft}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=draft}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=draft}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=draft}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=draft}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=draft}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=draft}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=draft}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|12=draft}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}

The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Engishiki Jinmyōchō|" to "Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=draft}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=draft}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=draft}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=draft}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=draft}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=draft}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=draft}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=draft}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=draft}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|12=draft}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}

In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}

revision.comment: 25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社 up to rev 88534900
revision.text:
{{神社 |画像=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
境内（石段奥に拝殿） |名称=葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]加守1045 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 51 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 18 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=[[天羽槌雄神|天羽雷命]] |創建=不詳 |社格=[[式内社]]（大）論社
旧[[村社]] |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |例祭=[[10月17日]] }} [[画像:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|境内入口}}]] '''葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社'''（かつらきしとりにいますあめのはいかづちのみことじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]の[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓にある[[神社]]である。[[式内大社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。単に'''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）とも呼ばれる。 == 祭神 == [[天羽槌雄神|天羽雷命]]（あまはいかづちのみこと）を主祭神とし、右殿に摂社・掃守神社（[[天忍人命]]）、左殿に摂社・二上神社（[[国魂|大国魂命]]）を配祀する。 天羽雷命は各地に機織や裁縫の技術を伝えた[[倭文氏]]の祖神で、当社は日本各地にある[[倭文神社]]の根本の神社とされる。 天忍人命は[[ウガヤフキアエズ|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊]]生誕のときにその胎便（「蟹」と呼ばれる）を掃除したという神で、その子孫は蟹守（かもり）氏（掃部、加守、狩森などとも）と称した。当社の社家も蟹守氏である。附近の「加守」という地名も蟹守に由来するもので、当社は江戸時代まで「加守明神」と呼ばれていた。 大国魂命は二上山山頂にある[[葛木二上神社]]の祭神である。山頂の二上神社の遥拝所もしくは里宮的な存在であったとみられる。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建の年代等は不詳である。文献の初出は、『[[日本三代実録]]』の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]条、当社に[[従五位|従五位上]]の[[神階]]を授けるという記述である。[[延喜式神名帳]]では「[[大和国]][[葛下郡]] 葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社」と記載され、大社に列している（ただし、葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社については葛城市新庄町の[[博西神社]]も論社とされている）。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列格し、明治40年（[[1907年]]）に神饌幣帛料供進社に指定された。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下から従五位上（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「葛木倭文天羽雷命神」。 == 境内 == ファイル:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|境内社 == 交通アクセス == *[[近畿日本鉄道]] [[南大阪線]] [[二上神社口駅]]から西へ徒歩10分。 == 関連項目 == *[[倭文神社]] *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきしとりにいますあめのはいかつちのみことしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきしとりにいますあめのはいかつちのみことしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=draft}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=draft}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=draft}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=draft}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=draft}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=draft}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=draft}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=draft}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=draft}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|12=draft}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}}

In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|式内社" to "|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{citation needed|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]
