revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Shikinaisha of" to "Shikinaisha in"

Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Shikinaisha (Major)]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|qq=}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|qq=}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Former Village Shrines" to "Son-sha"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmori Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanobito-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijo Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|qq=}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|qq=}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|qq=}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|qq=}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|qq=}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|qq=}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmori Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanobito-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijo Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|qq=}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|qq=}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|qq=}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|qq=}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|ja|二上神社口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijō Shrine-guchi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|qq=}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|qq=}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|qq=}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|qq=}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmori Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanobito-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijo Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|qq=}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|qq=}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|qq=}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|qq=}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]]

In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|qq=}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}, and in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|qq=}} (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]]
