Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373935}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|1=Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620953}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1907|Meiji 40]] (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11620953}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620953]] Wikidata name: Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620953]] Wikidata name: Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijo Shrine|1=Nijo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45261123}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373935}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|1=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620952}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|1=Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620953}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1907|Meiji 40]] (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11620953}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620953]] Wikidata name: Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620953]] Wikidata name: Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijo Shrine|1=Nijo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45261123}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373935}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|1=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620952}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|1=Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620953}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1907|Meiji 40]] (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11620953}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijo Shrine|1=Nijo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45261123}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373935}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|1=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620952}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|1=Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620953}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1907|Meiji 40]] (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11620953}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620953]] Wikidata name: Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620953]] Wikidata name: Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijo Shrine|1=Nijo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45261123}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373935}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|1=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620952}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|1=Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620953}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1907|Meiji 40]] (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11620953}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijo Shrine|1=Nijo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45261123}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373935}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|1=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620952}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|1=Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620953}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1907|Meiji 40]] (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11620953}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[commons:Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620953]] Wikidata name: Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620953]] Wikidata name: Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijo Shrine|1=Nijo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45261123}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373935}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|1=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620952}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|1=Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620953}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1907|Meiji 40]] (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - [[commons:Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[commons:Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620953]] Wikidata name: Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620953]] Wikidata name: Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | image=[[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds (Haiden beyond the stone steps) | name=Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine | location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara]], [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] 1045 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 31 | lat_seconds = 51 | N/S = N | longitude = 135 | long_minutes = 41 | long_seconds = 18 | E/W = E | country = Japan | map = Japan Nara | deity={{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} | established=Unknown | shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Major) Disputed | former_shrine_Rank={{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | main_hall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | annual_festival=[[October 17]] }} [[File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Sandou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine entrance}}]] '''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社, ''Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the base of [[:en:Mount_Nijō|Mount Nijo]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a historic {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and formerly held the status of a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to simply as '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shizuri Jinja''). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. The right hall houses the associated shrine {{ill|Sōmori Shrine|ja|掃守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to {{ill|Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanobito-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the left hall houses the associated {{ill|Nijo Shrine|ja|二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijo Shrine|1=Nijo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45261123}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}}. Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, who were known for spreading weaving and tailoring techniques across Japan. This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373935}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori''). The shrine’s priestly family, the Kamori clan, traces its origins to him. The local place name "Kamori" (加守) is derived from this lineage, and the shrine was historically called "Kamori Myōjin" until the Edo period. Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|ja|葛木二上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Nijo Shrine|1=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620952}}, which is located at the summit of Mount Nijo. It is believed that this shrine functioned either as a remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the mountaintop shrine or as its lower sanctuary. == History == == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown. The first literary mention appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (''The True Records of Three Generations of Japan'') in the first year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] (859), stating that the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|1=Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620953}}," classified as a major shrine. However, another shrine, {{ill|Hakusei Shrine|ja|博西神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja|葛城市新庄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō, Katsuragi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also a candidate for this designation. In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1907|Meiji 40]] (1907), it was designated as a shrine eligible for government offerings (''Shinsenheihaku-ryō kyōshinsha''). === Shrine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 3 (859), January 27: Promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''). The shrine was referred to as "{{ill|Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文天羽雷命神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." == Shrine Grounds == File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Katsuragishizurinimasu Jinja Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrine == Access == * 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Nijō Shrine-guchi Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近畿日本鉄道 南大阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22826 Katsuragi Shizurinimasu Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation" {{commons|Category:Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|version=88534900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] {{Commons category|Katuraki-shitoriniimasu-amenohaikaduchinomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11620953}} [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

This shrine is considered the head shrine of the various {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} throughout the country. Amanobito-no-Mikoto is a deity known for cleaning up the afterbirth (referred to as "crab") during the birth of {{ill|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja|彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373935}}. His descendants became the {{ill|Kamori clan|ja|蟹守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also written as ''Kamo-ri,'' ''Kamor,'' or ''Karimori'').
